text: public_key_types, private_key_types, certificate_private_key_types, certificate_issuer_public_key_types, certificate_public_key_types: Stop using the old uppercase type aliases; use the new capitalized names instead.
cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.modes: Stop importing CFB, OFB, and CFB8 from the standard module; import them from cryptography.hazmat.decrepit instead.
cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.algorithms: Stop importing Camellia from the standard module; import it from cryptography.hazmat.decrepit instead.
cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.algorithms: Stop importing CAST5, SEED, IDEA, or Blowfish from the standard module; import them from cryptography.hazmat.decrepit instead.
cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.algorithms: Stop importing TripleDES or ARC4 from the standard module; import them from cryptography.hazmat.decrepit instead.
csr.get_attribute_for_oid(oid): Stop calling get_attribute_for_oid directly on a CertificateSigningRequest; call it on the .attributes property instead.

ellipticcurvepublicnumbers.encode_point() and from_encoded_point(): Stop using encode_point and from_encoded_point on EllipticCurvePublicNumbers; use EllipticCurvePublicKey.public_bytes() and EllipticCurvePublicKey.from_encoded_point() instead.
Stop using signer() and verifier() on key objects; use sign() and verify() instead.
Stop using SECT* binary elliptic curve classes; they are completely removed with no replacement.
Stop using OpenSSL 1.1.x; OpenSSL 3.0.0 or later is now required.
md5 or sha1 hash algorithms in certificate builders: Stop using MD5 or SHA1 in certificate builders; they are no longer accepted.

meta.symbol: md5 or sha1 hash algorithms in certificate builders

text: Stop using Python 3.6, 3.7, or 3.8; Python 3.9 or later is required.

chacha20 treats first 4 bytes of nonce as a 32-bit little-endian block counter (rfc 7539) and raises error on counter overflow: ChaCha20 now interprets the first 4 bytes of the nonce as a 32-bit little-endian block counter per RFC 7539 and raises an error on counter overflow.

meta.symbol: chacha20 treats first 4 bytes of nonce as a 32-bit little-endian block counter (rfc 7539) and raises error on counter overflow

text: Loading an X.509 CRL with mismatched signature algorithms now raises ValueError during parsing instead of only during validation.
loaded with deprecation warning: Loading certificates with ECDSA/DSA NULL parameters in the signature now raises ValueError instead of a deprecation warning.
load_ssh_private_key() now raises TypeError if an unencrypted key is given a password, and TypeError if an encrypted key is given no password.
raised valueerror for invalid encoding: public_bytes and private_bytes now raise TypeError instead of ValueError for an invalid encoding.
returned empty list: Loading a PKCS7 structure with no content field now raises ValueError instead of returning an empty list.
rfc4514_string() now formats email addresses with the standard emailAddress attribute instead of the nonstandard E.
Loading keys with unsupported algorithms now raises UnsupportedAlgorithm instead of ValueError.
bpf_hash(name, ktype, vtype): Stop using BPF_HASH macro; define an explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) and SEC(".maps") annotation instead.
bpf_array(name, type, size): Stop using BPF_ARRAY macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY struct with SEC(".maps") annotation instead.
bpf_perf_output(name): Stop using BPF_PERF_OUTPUT macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY struct with SEC(".maps") annotation instead.
Stop using map.lookup(&key); call bpf_map_lookup_elem(&map, &key) instead.
Stop using map.update(&key, &val); call bpf_map_update_elem(&map, &key, &val, 0) instead.
Stop using events.perf_submit(ctx, data, len); call bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, data, len) instead.
implicit #include <linux/...> kernel headers: Stop relying on BCC's implicit kernel headers; include a single #include "vmlinux.h" instead.
You must now explicitly #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> and #include <bpf/bpf_core_read.h> in every BPF program.
Stop using direct kernel struct field access ptr->field->subfield; use BPF_CORE_READ(ptr, field, subfield) instead.
Stop using int kprobe__func(struct pt_regs *ctx, args); use SEC("kprobe/func") int BPF_KPROBE(kprobe__func, args) instead.
explicit sec("...") annotation on every program: You must now add an explicit SEC("...") annotation on every BPF program instead of relying on BCC's implicit naming.

meta.symbol: explicit sec("...") annotation on every program

text: bcc's compile-time #define config substitution: Stop using #define for config values in BPF programs; use global const volatile variables instead.
generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach(): Stop using BCC's runtime compilation; use a generated skeleton with name__open(), name__load(), and name__attach() instead.

meta.symbol: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()

text: Read global variables from userspace via skel->rodata, skel->bss, or skel->data instead of BCC's previous approach.
Stop using bpf_trace_printk(); use bpf_printk() instead, which has a 3-argument limit.
must be marked static __always_inline: All static helper functions in BPF programs must now be marked static __always_inline.
Stop using the ENGINE API; use providers instead.
evp_md_meth_new(), evp_cipher_meth_new(), evp_pkey_meth_new(): Stop using EVP_MD_meth_new(), EVP_CIPHER_meth_new(), and EVP_PKEY_meth_new(); implement a provider instead.
rsa_meth_new(), ec_key_method_new(): Stop using RSA_meth_new() and EC_KEY_METHOD_new(); implement a provider instead.
Stop using EVP_PKEY_set1_engine(); use providers instead.
fips_mode(), fips_mode_set(): Stop using FIPS_mode() and FIPS_mode_set(); use EVP_default_properties_is_fips_enabled() and the FIPS provider instead.
rsa_new(), rsa_free(), rsa_up_ref(): Stop using RSA_new(), RSA_free(), and RSA_up_ref(); use EVP_PKEY_new(), EVP_PKEY_free(), and EVP_PKEY_up_ref() instead.
dh_new(), dsa_new(), ec_key_new(): Stop using DH_new(), DSA_new(), and EC_KEY_new(); use EVP_PKEY_new() and the EVP high-level APIs instead.

aes_encrypt(), aes_decrypt(), aes_set_encrypt_key(): Stop using AES_encrypt(), AES_decrypt(), and AES_set_encrypt_key(); use EVP_EncryptInit_ex(), EVP_EncryptUpdate(), and EVP_EncryptFinal_ex() instead.
des_encrypt(), des_cbc_encrypt(), des_ecb_encrypt(): Stop using DES_encrypt(), DES_cbc_encrypt(), and DES_ecb_encrypt(); use the EVP cipher functions instead.
sha1_init(), sha1_update(), sha1_final(): Stop using SHA1_Init(), SHA1_Update(), and SHA1_Final(); use EVP_DigestInit_ex(), EVP_DigestUpdate(), and EVP_DigestFinal_ex() instead.
md5_init(), md5_update(), md5_final(): Stop using MD5_Init(), MD5_Update(), and MD5_Final(); use the EVP digest functions or EVP_Q_digest() instead.
rsa_get0_n(), rsa_get0_e(), rsa_get0_d(): Stop using RSA_get0_n(), RSA_get0_e(), and RSA_get0_d(); use EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param() or EVP_PKEY_get_params() instead.
dh_get0_p(), dh_get0_g(), dh_get0_key(): Stop using DH_get0_p(), DH_get0_g(), and DH_get0_key(); use EVP_PKEY_get_params() instead.
Stop using RSA_set0_key(); use EVP_PKEY_fromdata() instead.

evp_pkey_get0_rsa() returned a mutable pointer to the internal key: EVP_PKEY_get0_RSA() now returns a const pointer to a cached copy of a provider-managed key; treat it as read-only and do not expect modifications to propagate.

meta.symbol: evp_pkey_get0_rsa() returned a mutable pointer to the internal key

text: evp_pkey_get0_ec_key(), evp_pkey_get0_dsa(), evp_pkey_get0_dh() returned mutable pointers: EVP_PKEY_get0_EC_KEY(), EVP_PKEY_get0_DSA(), and EVP_PKEY_get0_DH() now return const pointers; treat them as read-only.

meta.symbol: evp_pkey_get0_ec_key(), evp_pkey_get0_dsa(), evp_pkey_get0_dh() returned mutable pointers

text: d2i_rsaprivatekey(), d2i_rsapublickey(): Stop using d2i_RSAPrivateKey() and d2i_RSAPublicKey(); use OSSL_DECODER_from_bio() instead.
Stop using PEM_read_RSAPrivateKey(); use PEM_read_bio_PrivateKey_ex() instead.
Stop using RSA_generate_key(); use EVP_PKEY_keygen() instead.
Stop using EC_KEY_generate_key(); use EVP_PKEY_generate() instead.
rsa_sign(), rsa_verify(): Stop using RSA_sign() and RSA_verify(); use EVP_DigestSign() and EVP_DigestVerify() instead.
ecdsa_sign(), ecdsa_verify(): Stop using ECDSA_sign() and ECDSA_verify(); use EVP_DigestSign() and EVP_DigestVerify() instead.
dh_compute_key(), ecdh_compute_key(): Stop using DH_compute_key() and ECDH_compute_key(); use EVP_PKEY_derive() instead.
hmac_init(), hmac_update(), hmac_final(): Stop using HMAC_Init(), HMAC_Update(), and HMAC_Final(); use EVP_Q_mac() or the EVP_MAC layer instead.
cmac_init(), cmac_update(), cmac_final(): Stop using CMAC_Init(), CMAC_Update(), and CMAC_Final(); use EVP_MAC_init(), EVP_MAC_update(), and EVP_MAC_final() instead.
Stop using the RAND_DRBG subsystem; use the EVP_RAND API instead.
rand_bytes(), rand_priv_bytes() without library context: RAND_bytes() and RAND_priv_bytes() may need a library context; use RAND_bytes_ex() and RAND_priv_bytes_ex() instead.
Stop using EVP_PKEY_set_alias_type() as it has been removed with no replacement.
bn_is_prime_ex() / bn_is_prime_fasttest_ex(): Stop using BN_is_prime_ex() and BN_is_prime_fasttest_ex(); use BN_check_prime() instead.
evp_sha256() / evp_aes_256_gcm(): EVP_sha256() and EVP_aes_256_gcm() may incur a performance penalty; fetch algorithms explicitly with EVP_MD_fetch() and EVP_CIPHER_fetch() instead.
SSL_CTX_new() may need SSL_CTX_new_ex() to pass a library context; using SSL_CTX_new() without a library context may silently misbehave.
dsa/dss key algorithm support: Stop using DSA/DSS keys; they are no longer supported.
sha-1 rsa signature verification: Stop using SHA-1 RSA signatures; they are no longer verified.
diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1, diffie-hellman-group14-sha1, diffie-hellman-group1-sha1: Stop using SHA-1 based Diffie-Hellman key exchange methods; they are removed.
gssapi support: Stop using GSSAPI authentication; it is no longer supported.
python 2 and python < 3.6 support: Stop using Python 2 or Python versions below 3.6; they are no longer supported.
paramiko.py3compat module: Stop importing paramiko.py3compat; the module is removed.
Stop using paramiko.common.asbytes; use paramiko.util.asbytes instead.
pkey.__cmp__ ordering comparison: Stop using PKey.__cmp__ for ordering comparisons; it is removed.
Stop using paramiko.util.retry_on_signal; it is removed.
Stop using paramiko.__all__; it is removed.
Stop using the ed25519, invoke, and all packaging extras; they are removed.

meta.symbol: ed25519, invoke, and all packaging extras

text: Stop using passphrase= in PKey.from_path; use password= instead.
proxycommand was deleted when not set: SSHConfig now sets proxycommand to None instead of deleting the key when no proxy is configured.

meta.symbol: proxycommand was deleted when not set

text: 1024 bits: The minimum DH group-exchange modulus is now 2048 bits; connections using 1024-bit groups will fail.
sha1 preferred: RSA public-key algorithms now prefer SHA2 over SHA1; SHA1-based RSA may stop working.
PKey.write_private_key methods were reorganized; child classes no longer override them, so custom subclasses will break.
support for libressl, wolfssl: Stop using urllib3 with LibreSSL or wolfSSL; only OpenSSL is supported.
Stop using urllib3 with OpenSSL older than 1.1.1; OpenSSL 1.1.1 or later is required.
Stop using urllib3 on Python 2.7 or Python 3.5–3.9; Python 3.10 or later is required.
google app engine, jython: Stop using urllib3 on Google App Engine or Jython; only CPython and PyPy3 are supported.
Stop importing urllib3.contrib.ntlmpool; it has been removed.
Stop importing urllib3.contrib.securetransport; it has been removed.
urllib3[secure] pip extra: Stop using pip install urllib3[secure]; that extra has been removed.
httpresponse.strict attribute and strict parameter: Stop accessing HTTPResponse.strict or passing the strict parameter; both are removed.
Stop importing VerifiedHTTPSConnection from urllib3.connectionpool; use HTTPSConnection from urllib3.connection instead.
The default minimum TLS version is now TLS 1.2 instead of TLS 1.0; connections to servers requiring TLS 1.0 or 1.1 will fail.
The default request body encoding is now UTF-8 instead of ISO-8859-1; non-UTF-8 byte sequences may cause errors or be misinterpreted.
commonname (cn) fallback for hostname verification: Stop relying on commonName for certificate hostname verification; only subjectAltName is used, so certificates without SAN will fail verification.
urllib3's own default cipher list: urllib3 no longer sets a default cipher list; it uses the system-configured ciphers, which may change which servers are connectable.
urls without a scheme (e.g. 'example.com/path') were accepted: Stop passing URLs without a scheme to urllib3; they are deprecated and will raise errors in a future version.

meta.symbol: urls without a scheme (e.g. 'example.com/path') were accepted

text: Stop implementing calculate(); implement run() instead.
Stop using render_text(); return a TreeGrid() from run() instead.
Stop using table_header() and table_row(); build a TreeGrid() with typed columns instead.
Stop inheriting from Volatility 2 base classes; inherit from interfaces.plugins.PluginInterface instead.
Stop using the --profile option; Volatility 3 auto-detects the OS and configures symbols via get_requirements().
Stop accessing addrspace directly; declare a Memory Layer in get_requirements() and access it through context.layers.

meta.symbol: memory layer via get_requirements() and context.layers

text: Stop defining plugin options via class inheritance or argparse; declare them in get_requirements() as requirements.* objects.
Stop using short_option for arguments; it is no longer available.
unescaped `{` outside repetition operator was allowed: Stop using unescaped `{` in regex outside repetition operators; escape it as `\{` or it will produce an error.

meta.symbol: unescaped `{` outside repetition operator was allowed

text: invalid escapes like `\g` treated as literal character: Stop using invalid regex escape sequences like `\g`; they now produce an error instead of being treated as literal characters.

meta.symbol: invalid escapes like `\g` treated as literal character

text: base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters were allowed: Stop using base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters; they now produce an error.

meta.symbol: base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters were allowed

text: `base64` and `base64wide` had to share the same alphabet: If you relied on `base64` and `base64wide` sharing the same alphabet, update your rules because they can now use different alphabets.

meta.symbol: `base64` and `base64wide` had to share the same alphabet

text: wildcard rule names like `1 of (rule_name*)` were valid: Stop using wildcard rule names in `of` statements like `1 of (rule_name*)`; they are no longer valid.

meta.symbol: wildcard rule names like `1 of (rule_name*)` were valid

text: xor applies before checking fullword delimiters: Stop relying on XOR being applied after fullword delimiter checks; in YARA-X XOR is applied before checking fullword delimiters, which changes matching behaviour.

meta.symbol: xor applies before checking fullword delimiters

text: negative array index like `@a[-1]` evaluated to undefined: Stop using negative array indices like `@a[-1]`; they now produce an error instead of evaluating to undefined.

meta.symbol: negative array index like `@a[-1]` evaluated to undefined

text: jump bounds accepted base-10 only: Stop assuming hex-pattern jump bounds only accept base-10 values; they now also accept hex and octal values, which may change parsing of existing rules.
duplicate `global`/`private` modifiers were allowed: Stop using duplicate `global` or `private` rule modifiers; each may now appear only once or it will produce an error.
